claim: Deploying AI apps to cloud requires reverse proxy setup (nginx) to map custom domains to localhost services running on specific ports
actionable: For cloud-deployed AI apps, configure nginx reverse proxy with proper firewall rules (ports 80/443) and SSL certificates to route custom domain to internal service ports.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST

claim: Docker Compose environment variables must be edited for database credentials before deployment; service names in docker-compose act as internal DNS hostnames
actionable: When deploying docker-compose stacks, reference container service names as hostnames (e.g., 'postgres' not 'localhost') and set DB credentials in .env before spin-up.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST

claim: Ollama models must be pre-pulled in docker-compose by modifying the container start command to avoid runtime delays waiting for model pulls
actionable: Pre-load Ollama models in docker-compose.yml by appending model pull commands to the container start instruction so models are ready at service startup.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST

claim: SSL certificates via Certbot require DNS A record pointing to cloud server IP; deployment workflow: firewall > nginx config > DNS > certbot > docker compose
actionable: Follow exact deployment sequence: enable UFW firewall with ports 80/443 open, configure nginx reverse proxy, create DNS A record, run certbot, then start containers.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST

claim: This deployment pattern (reverse proxy + docker-compose) applies universally to Streamlit, Next.js, or any service running on localhost with a port
actionable: Apply this cloud deployment architecture to any application type; change only the target localhost port and service configuration, not the fundamental nginx/SSL/DNS pattern.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST

claim: Open Code RCE vulnerability existed for extended period due to insecure server architecture by default before being discovered
actionable: When using open-source AI tools with server architectures, audit for RCE vulnerabilities before production deployment; prefer client-side or containerized approaches.
source_title: Deploy ANY AI App to the Cloud FAST
